ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for red bean xiao long bao. FOODON_03302050 (dumpling food product) is the closest defensible parent class covering steamed filled dumplings.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers steamed buns and dumplings as a category. No specific code exists for red bean xiao long bao; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific steamed dumpling product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant order of 10 steamed red bean xiao long bao pieces, weighing approximately 250 g in total (approximately 25 g per piece).

nutrient_source.title: Red Bean Paste Bun (Dou Sha Bao) — steamed, per 100 g; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Steamed Bun with Sweet Bean Paste Filling (SR Legacy) and HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database: Bao with Sweet Filling
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (FoodData Central SR Legacy) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo / Nutrient Composition Database)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for red bean xiao long bao (thin-skinned Shanghai-style) exists in HPB or USDA. Values are adapted from HPB Singapore data for steamed bao with sweet bean paste filling and cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy entry for steamed bun with sweet bean paste filling. Xiao long bao skins are thinner than standard bao, yielding slightly lower carbohydrate and energy per 100 g. Macros adjusted proportionally based on a filling-to-skin ratio of approximately 60:40 (red bean paste to dough). Values represent a best-estimate composite and are flagged as medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J; red bean / adzuki bean products and steamed wheat-based dumplings with sweet filling
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed journal (American Journal of Clinical Nutrition)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for red bean xiao long bao. GI estimated at 50 based on two analogous approved-source data points: (1) steamed wheat-based bao with sweet bean paste filling, GI approximately 53 (University of Sydney GI Database); (2) adzuki red bean paste products, GI approximately 43–50 (Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables). A composite midpoint of 50 is applied, classified as low GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 111.25) / 100 = 55.6, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 44.5 × 2.5 = 111.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~115 mg per 100 g; ~288 mg per 10-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: A 10-piece serving contributes approximately 288 mg sodium, which is within a manageable range for hypertensive individuals when consumed as part of a low-sodium overall diet; however, portion control is advised if additional high-sodium dishes are consumed in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate load per serving (approximately 111 g per 10-piece order) despite low GI (50)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index of approximately 50 is in the low range due to the red bean paste filling and steamed wheat dough, the large carbohydrate load per standard 10-piece serving produces a high glycemic load (~55.6), making portion reduction essential for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes managing postprandial glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), no cholesterol from filling, and dietary fibre from red bean paste (~3.2 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The red bean filling provides dietary fibre and negligible saturated fat, making this a relatively heart-friendly dim sum option; individuals with cardiovascular disease should still moderate portion size to manage total caloric and carbohydrate intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Red Bean Xiao Long Bao (豆沙小笼包) is a sweet dessert variant of the classic Shanghai-style xiao long bao (soup dumpling), in which the traditional pork-and-gelatin filling is replaced with finely mashed, sweetened red bean (adzuki bean) paste. The dough skin is thin, translucent, and smooth — characteristic of the xiao long bao style — and is pleated at the top in the traditional manner. The dumpling is steamed in bamboo baskets and served hot. The texture is soft and yielding, with a slightly chewy dough exterior and a dense, smooth, mildly sweet red bean interior. Unlike the savoury original, this version contains no soup broth inside. It is a popular dessert or sweet dim sum item offered at premium Shanghainese restaurants in Singapore, most notably Din Tai Fung. Nutritionally, each piece weighs approximately 25 g and provides around 54 kcal, with carbohydrate as the dominant macronutrient (~44.5 g per 100 g) and modest protein (~5.2 g per 100 g) from the wheat dough and red bean. The red bean paste contributes dietary fibre (~3.2 g per 100 g) and trace iron. Fat content is very low (~1.8 g per 100 g) and saturated fat is minimal (~0.4 g per 100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 50 (low GI), but the glycemic load per standard 10-piece serving is high (~55.6) due to the large carbohydrate mass, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium is moderate at ~115 mg per 100 g. This dish is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable, and contains gluten from the wheat dough skin.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for red bean xiao long bao exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from the closest approved-source analogues: HPB steamed bao with sweet bean paste filling and USDA SR Legacy steamed bun with sweet bean paste. GI is estimated from analogous approved-source data (University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021). Medium confidence is appropriate given the adaptation required.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on HPB Singapore (steamed bao with sweet bean paste) and USDA SR Legacy (steamed bun with sweet bean paste filling); no exact match for thin-skinned xiao long bao with red bean filling exists in approved primary sources. 2. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (steamed wheat bao with sweet filling, GI ~53) and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (adzuki bean paste products, GI 43–50); classified as low GI. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: red bean paste and wheat dough contain no cholesterol; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plant-based steamed bun with sweet filling. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: steaming destroys residual vitamin C in red bean; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for comparable product. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero: this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero by food composition. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero: no hydrogenated fats used in traditional steamed dough or red bean paste; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source databases for this food category. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA SR Legacy entries used for adaptation. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted sources for this product category. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases; plant-based steamed product with no vitamin D fortification. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source databases for this food. 12. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source databases for this food. 13. Serving size of 250 g (10 pieces at ~25 g each) is estimated from typical Din Tai Fung xiao long bao piece weight; exact restaurant weight not confirmed by approved source. 14. Cuisine tag 'Noodles' in the input is not applicable to this product and has been removed. 15. FoodOn FOODON_03302050 and FoodEx2 A0FKP are closest_parent matches; no exact ontology codes exist for this specific product.
